title: NFT Burning Logic Incorrectly Calculates Burn Amount Leading to Token/NFT Loss
reason: The cited formula in `_update` correctly counts how many whole `tokensPerNFT` thresholds the sender crossed during an ERC20 transfer; by itself it does not miscompute burns.

title: Max Wallet Limit Can Be Bypassed via NFT Transfer Functions
reason: `safeTransferFrom` and `safeBatchTransferFrom` both reach `_afterTokenTransfer`, and the `ERC_X` override enforces the `maxWallet` check there.

title: Transfer Delay Can Be Bypassed via NFT Transfer Functions
reason: The NFT transfer paths also hit `_afterTokenTransfer`; the supported issue is the opposite one, where the delay hook can self-revert certain ERC20 transfers.

title: _burnBatch Search from _nextTokenId() Fails When Tokens Have Been Burned
reason: `findLastSet(before)` is a backward scan, so starting from `_nextTokenId()` and then updating `searchFrom = id` correctly walks down previously owned token ids even when gaps exist.

title: ERC20/NFT ID Space Collision Creates Ambiguity
reason: Too generic as stated; the concrete, reportable consequences are already captured by the approval-confusion and broken NFT `transferFrom` findings.

title: Missing Verification of NFT Ownership Before transferFrom NFT Path
reason: Unsupported speculation: the path checks `_owned[from].get(value)` before proceeding, and no concrete race or reentrancy-based exploit was demonstrated.
